channel.title: News & Events on Global Commerce and Services
channel.description: Recent content in News & Events on Global Commerce and Services

item.title: Navy & Marine Corps Procurement Conference
item.description: GCS is proud to serve as a Small Business Sponsor for the 2026 Navy & Marine Corps Procurement Conference, a premier defense industry event focused on connecting government and industry leaders to support evolving Navy and Marine Corps mission requirements, procurement priorities, and modernization initiatives. We look forward to engaging with federal partners, prime contractors, and industry peers to support mission readiness, innovation, and operational excellence across the Department of the Navy.

item.title: GSA Awarded Missile Defense Agency (MDA) Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) Contract
item.description: GCS is pleased to announce that it was awarded a contract for the Missile Defense Agency Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity (IDIQ) contract with a ceiling of $151B. This contract encompasses a broad range of work areas that allows for the rapid delivery of innovative capabilities to the warfighter with increased speed and agility.

item.title: GCS Achieves ISO 9001:2015 Recertification
item.description: GCS is pleased to announce it has successfully achieved recertification to the ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management System (QMS) standard, reaffirming the company’s commitment to delivering consistent, high-quality services to its federal customers. ISO 9001:2015 is an internationally recognized standard that establishes requirements for effective quality management, continuous improvement, and customer satisfaction. GCS’s recertification reflects the strength and maturity of its internal processes, governance, and performance management practices. Maintaining ISO 9001:2015 certification ensures that GCS consistently applies proven processes across program management, service delivery, and operational support—enabling the company to meet evolving mission needs while maintaining the highest standards of quality and performance.

item.title: GCS and Nationwide IT Services Launch Mentor-Protégé Joint Venture: GCNIS
item.description: GCS and Nationwide IT Services (NIS) are proud to announce the formation of GCNIS, a new Small Business Administration (SBA) Mentor-Protégé joint venture established to deliver innovative, secure, and cost-effective IT solutions to federal customers. GCNIS is a HUBZone and Economically Disadvantaged Woman-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB). The joint venture brings together the complementary strengths of two proven organizations to serve as a single, accountable partner for enterprise IT modernization. NIS contributes deep expertise in cloud computing, software development, and advanced analytics, while GCS provides robust capabilities in IT infrastructure, networking, and cybersecurity. Together, GCNIS delivers end-to-end IT solutions that support mission-critical operations across the federal enterprise. GCNIS combines the scale, experience, and technical depth of a large business with the agility, responsiveness, and customer focus of a small business. This model enables the joint venture to rapidly adapt to evolving mission needs while maintaining the highest standards of quality, security, and performance. “Our partnership reflects a shared commitment to delivering measurable value to government customers,” said leadership from GCS and NIS. “By uniting our capabilities, GCNIS is uniquely positioned to help agencies modernize their IT environments while controlling costs and reducing risk.” GCNIS remains committed to supporting its customers, employees, and the Nation, reinforcing a shared vision to serve as a trusted advisor and technical solutions partner across government programs. For more information about GCNIS and its capabilities, please visit https://www.nw-its.com/gcnis

item.title: GCS Awarded GSA OASIS+ Contract in Management & Advisory Domain
item.description: GCS is proud to announce it has been awarded a contract for the General Services Administration (GSA) One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) contract vehicle in the Management & Advisory domain under both the HUBZone and Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) tracks. OASIS+ is GSA’s premier governmentwide, multi-award IDIQ contract designed to streamline the acquisition of complex professional services across federal agencies. GCS’s selection reflects the company’s proven expertise in delivering high-quality management consulting, program and project management, strategic planning, and operational support services to mission-critical government programs. “This award underscores GCS’s commitment to providing customer-focused, results-driven management and advisory solutions,” said Joaneane Smith-Jones, President and CEO. “Being selected under both the HUBZone and WOSB tracks highlights our dedication to excellence, compliance, and meaningful socioeconomic impact while supporting federal missions.” Through the OASIS+ Management & Advisory domain, GCS will support federal customers with services including: • Program and project management support • Strategic planning and organizational transformation • Acquisition and financial management support • Process improvement and performance management • Policy analysis and advisory services The OASIS+ contract vehicle provides federal agencies with a flexible, efficient procurement pathway to access GCS’s capabilities while meeting small business, HUBZone, and WOSB contracting goals. This award further strengthens GCS’s position as a trusted advisor and solutions partner to the federal government, reinforcing the company’s mission to deliver innovative, cost-effective, and high-impact services to its customers and the Nation. For more information about GCS’s OASIS+ capabilities, please visit [OASIS+ Capabilities or link].
